---
title: "FW 536 — Day 1 morning lab (v2 · marine & fisheries) — Your Name"
author: "Your Name Here"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    self_contained: true
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 3
    toc_float: true
    code_folding: show
    theme: cosmo
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE)
```

# Day 1 morning lab — Probability problem set

**Problem set version:** **v2 — marine & fisheries theme**.
(The companion version is [v3 — terrestrial wildlife & disease](problem_set_v3.html); you choose whichever theme fits your interests.)

This template matches [`problem_set_v2.html`](problem_set_v2.html). Read each problem statement there, write your work below, **Knit** to HTML, and upload the `.html` to the matching Canvas assignment.

For setup help see the [R Markdown tutorial](../rmarkdown_tutorial.html).

---

# Setup

```{r}
# Load packages you'll need. Add or remove as appropriate.
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
```

---

# Problem 1 — Acoustic detection of tagged steelhead

> A tagged steelhead passes through a river reach with two acoustic receivers. Each receiver, independently and given the fish is present, detects the tag with probability 0.85. If the fish is absent, false detections are negligible.

**My work:**

```{r}
# Your R code here
```

**Answer / biological interpretation:**


---

# Problem 2 — eDNA detection of invasive carp

> In a lake, the prior probability that bighead carp are present is 0.20. An eDNA assay has sensitivity 0.92 (P(+|present)) and specificity 0.97 (P(−|absent)).

**My work:**

```{r}
# Your R code here
```

**Answer / biological interpretation:**


---

# Problem 3 — Sea turtle nest success

> A monitored loggerhead beach has $n=18$ nests, each with hatching success probability $p = 0.62$ (assume independence).

**My work:**

```{r}
# Your R code here
```

**Answer / biological interpretation:**


---

# Problem 4 — Sea otter neighborhood survival

> $n = 20$ tagged sea otters in a kelp forest each survive a year with probability 0.9 independently. Count the number of "isolated mortality" events — an otter that died, whose immediate two neighbors (by tag ID order) both lived.

**My work:**

```{r}
# Your R code here
```

**Answer / biological interpretation:**


---

# Problem 5 — Albatross bycatch in a longline fishery

> Bycatch events of black-footed albatross occur at rate $\lambda = 0.4$ per 1000 hooks set.

**My work:**

```{r}
# Your R code here
```

**Answer / biological interpretation:**


---

# (Optional) Embed a photo of hand-written work

For problems easier to do on paper, photograph and embed:

```{r, echo=FALSE, eval=FALSE, out.width="60%", fig.cap="Hand-derivation for Problem N"}
knitr::include_graphics("images/problemN_derivation.jpg")
```

(Change `eval=FALSE` to `eval=TRUE` after saving an image. See the [R Markdown tutorial](../rmarkdown_tutorial.html) for details.)

---

# Submission checklist

- [ ] Answered every morning problem from `problem_set_v2.html`.
- [ ] Knitted to HTML without errors.
- [ ] Photos (if any) embedded with `self_contained: true` in the YAML.
- [ ] Uploaded the `.html` to the matching Canvas assignment.
